reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: The demo runs a REAL in-browser document-question-answering pass: worker.js imports the shared loadPipeline (lib/webai.js) and calls pipe(imageURL, question, {max_new_tokens, streamer}) on the document-question-answering pipeline — Donut is OCR-FREE (Swin encoder over the whole document image → BART-style decoder), conditioned on a deterministic task prompt <s_docvqa><s_question>…</s_question><s_answer>. No canned result. Grounded in the actual worker source + the immutable conformance suite, which tests `loads-clean` (console-clean, headless Chrome) and `calls-real-inference` (source pattern: pipeline(|createModelLoader|...). The exact decoder prompt is built in code (buildPrompt) and shown verbatim in See-inside; the answer is first?.answer ?? generated_text from the real generate() — never a fabricated field. Token streaming is best-effort (try/catch) and the code falls back to the real final answer if the streamer is unavailable. NOTE: this is a retrospective code+suite-grounded score — no fresh measured latency/screenshot was captured in this backfill pass, hence 4 not 5.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Web Worker (new Worker(worker.js, {type:'module'})); buildPrompt embeds the question; a best-effort TextStreamer streams answer tokens with timestamps; the final answer is always the real pipeline output.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = a direct plain-text answer to a plain-English question about a document (total, date, id…), generated OCR-free. The page shows the answer forming, the verbatim task prompt, and per-token timing in See-inside; the document image is displayed. No box overlay — the output is the answer text.
rubric.notes: Donut answers directly (no OCR intermediate); answers are short extractive-style fields. Token streaming is best-effort — if the pipeline ignores the streamer, the page still resolves with the real final answer (no fake trace).

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/donut-base-finetuned-docvqa, linked to its HF card), the real pipeline task (document-question-answering), backend (WASM — no WebGPU needed), quantisation (q8), download size (~215 MB) and license (MIT). The worker's loadPipeline call and the createModelLoader model config both use the SAME id/dtype/sizeMB — no invented API surface.

The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model:{modelId:'Xenova/donut-base-finetuned-docvqa', runtime:'transformers.js', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:215, requiresWebGPU:false}) — no hand-rolled Load button. A valid current on-device model auto-initialises (checking → initialising → ready); only Download/Re-download/Update surface otherwise. Conformance tests `shared-auto-init-loader` (source: createModelLoader).
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on Xenova/donut-base-finetuned-docvqa so the ~215 MB cache is tracked against the never-silently-re-download guarantee.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/inference state is announced in role=status aria-live regions (2 aria-live=polite regions in the page). Failures surface on the page as a readable `.status.err` message ("Answer failed: …"), never only in the console; the shared loader exposes Retry/re-download recovery. Runs on the WASM path everywhere — no WebGPU gate. Conformance tests `live-status-region`, `errors-on-the-page`.
rubric.notes: The verbatim task prompt is posted before generation; the answer streams in; a graceful fall-back covers a streamer-less path.

rubric.evidence: Real keyboard-operable controls: file dropzone (role=button), sample document thumbnails (role=button), a labelled question text input (aria-label='Question about the document', default 'What is the total due?'), a max-tokens slider and an Ask button.

No canned/hardcoded output: every visible result is derived from the real worker run — the answer is the real generate() output for the built task prompt; streamed tokens are real, with an honest fall-back to the final answer if streaming is unavailable. Conformance carries `no-fake-output` (manual-evidenced) and `calls-real-inference`.

The question input has aria-label='Question about the document'; sample thumbnails have alt text; the Ask button and max-tokens slider are named/labelled; status uses role=status aria-live; the answer is selectable text. Conformance tests `images-have-alt` and `buttons-have-names` (both script assertions over the DOM).
rubric.notes: models.json records support.desktop/mobile = needs-review with an automated pre-scan flag of several sub-24px tap targets (sample thumbnails / small chips) 'agent to verify tap size' — a genuine open item, not yet manually confirmed.

rubric.evidence: Layout uses the shared design system (public/styles.css) with fluid flex/grid, flex-wrap and intrinsic sizing; conformance carries `responsive-desktop` and `responsive-mobile` (no-horizontal-overflow at ≈1280×800 and ≈360×740 DPR3 touch) plus `parity-both-classes`.
rubric.notes: support.desktop/mobile are still needs-review in models.json (awaiting an agent parity verdict); models.json records support.desktop/mobile = needs-review with an automated pre-scan flag of several sub-24px tap targets (sample thumbnails / small chips) 'agent to verify tap size' — a genuine open item, not yet manually confirmed.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Web Worker (new Worker(worker.js, {type:'module'})); The Swin→BART generate() runs in the worker and streams tokens incrementally (best-effort) so the answer appears progressively rather than blocking. No main-thread inference or unbounded main-thread loop — INP stays low. Conformance carries `inference-off-main-thread` (source) and `inp-stays-low`.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status role status aria-live; every control keyboard-operable with a name
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; keep all controls keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the question field carries an explicit aria-label, the Ask button + slider are named, role=status aria-live announces state, and the answer is selectable text.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: aria-label on question input + live-status-region + buttons-have-names assertions

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low off main thread heavy work in a web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid long blocking tasks on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the OCR-free Swin→BART generate() runs in worker.js and streams answer tokens incrementally (best-effort), so the UI updates progressively and never blocks on the full decode.

guidanceConsulted.query: break up long tasks keep INP low off main thread web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break heavy synchronous processing out of the main thread (or yield) so the browser can handle input and repaint.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — heavy generation is entirely off the main thread; the page only handles small incremental token messages.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread; break-up-long-tasks principle honoured via the worker + streaming

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive layout no horizontal overflow fluid grid intrinsic sizing
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with wrap + intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — fluid flex/grid; the document image + answer wrap without horizontal overflow.

openQuestions: Donut gives short extractive answers with no confidence/score — is surfacing decoder token probabilities (or an abstain signal) worth an additive See-inside upgrade?
No fresh measured run captured here; a headless run citing a real answer (e.g. on sample-invoice.png) + latency would let real-inference score 5. Sub-24px tap targets also need manual verification.

followUpGoals.goal: Capture a retained headless run citing a real Donut answer + per-token timing so real-inference can be evidenced at 5.
Surface decoder token probabilities / an abstain signal in See-inside so answer confidence is legible.

summary: Retrospective code+conformance-grounded critique for donut-docvqa (Xenova/donut-base-finetuned-docvqa, document-question-answering, q8, ~215 MB, MIT). A real OCR-free document-VQA run off-thread: a deterministic task prompt (shown verbatim) conditions a Swin→BART generate() that answers directly, with best-effort token streaming and an honest fall-back to the real final answer. Labelled question input, shared auto-init loader, live status + on-page errors. Honest weaknesses: short answers with no confidence signal, streaming is best-effort, no fresh measured run captured here (real-inference 4), sub-24px tap-target flags at needs-review.
